key_dungeons.name: Rotting Barrow
key_dungeons.description: The air around Rotting Barrow shimmers with faint heat. Strange runes glow along the doorframe.
key_dungeons.quest_objective: Descend into Rotting Barrow and find the Iron Key
key_dungeons.quest_hint: The passage grows more treacherous with every floor.

game_log: Thelma: Welcome, friends — and forgive the mess. My cellar has been overrun with giant rats, and the noise from below has gotten worse the last few nights. I would handle it myself, but my husband is no fighter and neither am I. Clear them out and I can spare a set of camping supplies and a quiver of arrows for your trouble. Mind your steps down there — I do not think it is only rats anymore.
Thelma: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!
Quest 'A Rat Problem': Clear the rat from the shop floor - Complete!
Quest 'A Rat Problem': Hunt down the rat swarm in the basement tunnel - Complete!
Quest 'A Rat Problem': Deal with the goblin runner sniffing around - Complete! | All steps done! Return to the quest giver for your reward.
Thelma: Thank you, hero! Here is your reward: +100 XP, +100 Gold, +2 items (Camping Supplies, Arrows)

Neil: I am not — I am not really a ghost-hunter. We took the villagers' coin and waved a censer and that was the whole trade. Then in the second house, something rose up out of the floorboards and it was not smoke. Maric never came out. I cannot go back in there. Please — put whatever it is back in the ground and I will split what I have left.
Neil: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!
Quest 'Whatever Killed Maric': Put whatever killed Maric back in the ground - Complete! | All steps done! Return to the quest giver for your reward.
Neil: Thank you, hero! Here is your reward: +100 XP, +50 Gold
The party rests using Camping Supplies...
Restored 13 HP and 18 MP across the party!
The party rests using Camping Supplies...
Restored 0 HP and 14 MP across the party!

Loren: There is a cave in the cliffs north of here. We used to give it a wide berth and it gave us the same. Now they are raiding the high pastures, and we have found tracks in the orchard. The wards must be weakening for them to come this far. We will pay any adventurer brave enough to root them out — and a little more for whoever takes their war-leaders down with them.
Loren: [Adventurer's Note: This quest will take you into the Goblin's Nest dungeon — tread carefully.]
Loren: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!

Elder Wenryn: You feel it, do you not? The pressure on the air. Our village rests on a Sealstone — an old ward laid by the Conclave of Suns when this land was first settled. Three nights ago someone took it. The trail leads west, into the Old Forest by the Sea, and only the dead and the dragon's friends walk willingly into that wood. Bring it back to me, and I can show you a path off the island that the dragon's eyes do not yet watch.
Elder Wenryn: [Adventurer's Note: This quest will take you into the The Old Forest by the Sea dungeon — tread carefully. A powerful guardian is said to watch over what you seek.]
Elder Wenryn: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!

Lori: My husband ran back into our home when the dragon's shadow came over Shanty Town. He was carrying his grandfather's writ — a Veyron family scroll, sealed in red wax. He never came out. The fire took the house and what little of him we found, we buried. The scroll was not with him. There is an old place up the road, the abandoned merchant house, and locals say things have been moving in there since the wards weakened. I think he made it that far. I have no coin, but I have his last bottle of fire-oil. Please.  [ENTER]
Lori: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck!
The party defeated Goblin Raiding Party.
Lori: Thank you, hero! Here is your reward: +200 XP, +200 Gold, +Fire Oil  [ENTER]

Quill: I have been a grave-digger forty years. I have never had to put the same man in the ground twice — until last week. We are past three now. The crypt up on the bluffs is the source. Something in the seal-stones underneath has gone quiet, and the long-dead can feel it. I will pay what I have to anyone willing to go in there and put them down for good.
Quill: [Adventurer's Note: This quest will take you into the Crypt dungeon — tread carefully.]
Quill: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!
Welcome to Seat of the Realm!

Welcome to Seat of the Realm!

Laird Marrowen: Sit. Listen. The dragon you came here to slay is no random terror — Hargorn was bound by the Conclave of Suns a thousand years ago, sealed beneath the western water. The wards held until one of our own, Vorkhaal, learned to feed on them to extend his life. The first seal he broke woke the dragon. We knew this day might come, so the Conclave forged a failsafe: a blade quenched in the dragon's own heart-fire. The Sun Sword. It rests in the lowest citadel of the Sea Shrine, beneath the flood-line. Vorkhaal's wyrm-blooded cultists already crawl those tiers. Bring it back to me, and I will teach you to wield it.
Laird Marrowen: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!
The party defeated Troll and Wolves.

Captain Roland: I have lost three patrols in the last month. The orcs in the western hills used to be a nuisance; now they have a warchief, and warchiefs need war. We cannot hold the passes if they come in numbers. Get into the stronghold, break their footmen, and put a sword through whatever they are calling a king. The realm will pay, and pay well.
Captain Roland: [Adventurer's Note: This quest will take you into the Orc Stronghold dungeon — tread carefully.]
Captain Roland: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!

Branwen the Apostate: I was Conclave once. Before Vorkhaal inverted us. I left when I understood what he had done — and what he meant to keep doing. There is a scroll in the heart of the coven stronghold: the binding-rite, the words that let a sun-forged blade pierce a sealed-thing's hide. Without it, your Sun Sword is a fancy hilt. Go in. Take it. Vorkhaal's apprentices will show you no mercy, and I would ask you to show them none either.  [ENTER] >
Branwen the Apostate: [Adventurer's Note: This quest will take you into the Mage Coven dungeon — tread carefully. A powerful guardian is said to watch over what you seek.]  [ENTER]
Branwen the Apostate: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck!

Welcome to Seat of the Realm!

Queen Aelyse Veyron: You have done what I could not ask of any soldier of mine. You have walked into Vorkhaal's house, and into the drowned shrine, and come back. Now there is one task left. Hargorn rises every night, and the coast burns a little longer each time. Take the Sun Sword. Take the binding rite. Cross the strait to the dragon's island, and end this. Whatever the wyrm took from this realm, it is time we took it back.
Queen Aelyse Veyron: [Adventurer's Note: This quest will take you into the Dragon's Lair dungeon — tread carefully.]
Queen Aelyse Veyron: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!
